data.content:
### Basic useQuery Hook in React Source: https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/queries.md Demonstrates the fundamental usage of the `useQuery` hook from '@tanstack/react-query'. It takes a `queryKey` and a `queryFn` to fetch data. The hook returns an object containing query states and data. ```tsx import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'; function App() { const info = useQuery({ queryKey: ['todos'], queryFn: fetchTodoList }); } ``` -------------------------------- ### Define a basic custom React Query hook Source: https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/testing.md This TypeScript example defines `useCustomHook`, a simple custom hook that wraps `useQuery`. It fetches a static string 'Hello' with a predefined `queryKey`, serving as a base for unit testing demonstrations. ```tsx export function useCustomHook() { return useQuery({ queryKey: ['customHook'], queryFn: () => 'Hello' }) } ``` -------------------------------- ### Use useQueries with queries object (React Query / TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/migrating-to-react-query-4.md Demonstrates the new useQueries API which accepts a single object with a queries array. Dependency: React Query useQueries hook. Input: array of query option objects; Output: array of query results. Limitation: this replaces the previous positional array form. ```TypeScript ;-useQueries([ { queryKey1, queryFn1, options1 }, { queryKey2, queryFn2, options2 }, ]) + // [!code --] useQueries({ queries: [ { queryKey1, queryFn1, options1 }, { queryKey2, queryFn2, options2 }, ], }) // [!code ++] ``` -------------------------------- ### Execute Multiple Parallel Queries with useQueries() in React Query Source: https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/migrating-to-react-query-3.md This example shows how to use the `useQueries()` hook to perform multiple queries in parallel, which is useful when the number of queries is dynamic or determined at runtime. It takes an array of query configurations and returns an array of results, enabling efficient data fetching for lists or related items. ```tsx import { useQueries } from 'react-query' function Overview() { const results = useQueries([ { queryKey: ['post', 1], queryFn: fetchPost }, { queryKey: ['post', 2], queryFn: fetchPost }, ]) return (
    {results.map(({ data }) => data &&
  • {data.title})
    • )}
) } ``` -------------------------------- ### Basic Usage of useSuspenseQueries Hook (React Query/TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/reference/useSuspenseQueries.md Demonstrates the basic invocation of the `useSuspenseQueries` hook in TanStack Query. This hook allows you to fetch multiple queries simultaneously with Suspense, similar to `useQueries` but with specific adaptations for Suspense mode. Key differences include the inability to use `suspense`, `throwOnError`, `enabled`, or `placeholderData` options for individual queries, and guaranteed `data` definition in the return value. Components using this hook will re-mount only after all queries complete. ```tsx const result = useSuspenseQueries(options) ``` -------------------------------- ### Basic useQueries Implementation Source: https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/reference/useQueries.md Demonstrates how to use useQueries to fetch multiple posts by their IDs with individual query configurations ```tsx const ids = [1, 2, 3] const results = useQueries({ queries: ids.map((id) => ({ queryKey: ['post', id], queryFn: () => fetchPost(id), staleTime: Infinity, })), }) ``` -------------------------------- ### Define useQuery Hook with Options and Returned Values (TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/reference/useQuery.md This code snippet illustrates the destructuring of properties returned by the `useQuery` hook, such as data, error status, loading states, and refetch functions. It also showcases the primary configuration options available for customizing query behavior, including `queryKey`, `queryFn`, `gcTime`, `enabled`, and various refetching strategies. ```typescript const { data, dataUpdatedAt, error, errorUpdatedAt, failureCount, failureReason, fetchStatus, isError, isFetched, isFetchedAfterMount, isFetching, isInitialLoading, isLoading, isLoadingError, isPaused, isPending, isPlaceholderData, isRefetchError, isRefetching, isStale, isSuccess, isEnabled, promise, refetch, status, } = useQuery( { queryKey, queryFn, gcTime, enabled, networkMode, initialData, initialDataUpdatedAt, meta, notifyOnChangeProps, placeholderData, queryKeyHashFn, refetchInterval, refetchIntervalInBackground, refetchOnMount, refetchOnReconnect, refetchOnWindowFocus, retry, retryOnMount, retryDelay, select, staleTime, structuralSharing, subscribed, throwOnError, }, queryClient, ) ``` -------------------------------- ### Define a custom hook for network call testing Source: https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/testing.md This TypeScript function `useFetchData` illustrates a custom hook designed to make a network request using `useQuery`. It fetches data from `/api/data`, providing an example of a hook whose network interactions can be tested and mocked. ```tsx function useFetchData() { return useQuery({ queryKey: ['fetchData'], queryFn: () => request('/api/data'), }) } ``` -------------------------------- ### Manual Parallel Queries with useQuery (TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/guides/parallel-queries.md Demonstrates how to execute multiple `useQuery` hooks in parallel within a React component. These queries will run concurrently without any special setup, provided their keys and functions are distinct. This approach is suitable when the number of queries is fixed. ```tsx function App () { // The following queries will execute in parallel const usersQuery = useQuery({ queryKey: ['users'], queryFn: fetchUsers }) const teamsQuery = useQuery({ queryKey: ['teams'], queryFn: fetchTeams }) const projectsQuery = useQuery({ queryKey: ['projects'], queryFn: fetchProjects }) ... } ``` -------------------------------- ### useQueries with Combine Option Source: https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/reference/useQueries.md Shows how to combine multiple query results into a single object with custom properties ```tsx const ids = [1, 2, 3] const combinedQueries = useQueries({ queries: ids.map((id) => ({ queryKey: ['post', id], queryFn: () => fetchPost(id), })), combine: (results) => { return { data: results.map((result) => result.data), pending: results.some((result) => result.isPending), } }, }) ``` -------------------------------- ### TanStack Query Hook Return Properties Source: https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/reference/useQuery.md This section details the properties and methods returned by a TanStack Query hook (e.g., `useQuery`), which provide comprehensive information about the query's state, data, and error status, along with actions like refetching. ```APIDOC ## TanStack Query State & Actions (Hook Return Values) ### Description This documentation describes the comprehensive set of properties and methods returned by a TanStack Query hook (e.g., `useQuery`). These values provide real-time information about the query's current state, fetched data, error status, and offer functionalities like manual refetching. ### Context Output object from a TanStack Query hook (e.g., `useQuery`). ### Hook Options These are optional input parameters that can be provided when configuring the query hook. - **queryClient** (QueryClient) - Optional - Use this to provide a custom `QueryClient` instance for this specific query. If not provided, the `QueryClient` from the nearest context will be used. ### Returned Properties These are the properties and methods available on the object returned by the query hook, reflecting its current state and capabilities. - **status** (QueryStatus) - The current lifecycle status of the query. - `pending`: No cached data, and no query attempt has finished yet. - `error`: The most recent query attempt resulted in an error. - `success`: The query has successfully received a response with no errors. Also `success` if `enabled` is `false` and `initialData` was supplied. - **isPending** (boolean) - `true` if the `status` is currently `pending`. - **isSuccess** (boolean) - `true` if the `status` is currently `success`. - **isError** (boolean) - `true` if the `status` is currently `error`. - **isLoadingError** (boolean) - `true` if the query failed during its very first fetch attempt. - **isRefetchError** (boolean) - `true` if the query failed during a subsequent refetch attempt. - **data** (TData) - The last successfully resolved data for the query. Defaults to `undefined`. - **dataUpdatedAt** (number) - A Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) indicating when the query last transitioned to a `"success"` status. - **error** (null | TError) - The error object if the query encountered an error. Defaults to `null`. - **errorUpdatedAt** (number) - A Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) indicating when the query last transitioned to an `"error"` status. - **isStale** (boolean) - `true` if the data in the cache is currently invalidated or is older than the configured `staleTime`. - **isPlaceholderData** (boolean) - `true` if the data currently being displayed is placeholder data (e.g., from `placeholderData` option). - **isFetched** (boolean) - `true` if the query has completed at least one fetch attempt. - **isFetchedAfterMount** (boolean) - `true` if the query has completed a fetch attempt after the component was mounted. Useful for conditionally displaying data. - **fetchStatus** (FetchStatus) - The current status of the query's fetching process. - `fetching`: The `queryFn` is currently executing (applies to initial fetches and background refetches). - `paused`: The query intended to fetch but is currently paused (e.g., due to network issues). - `idle`: The query is not actively fetching. - **isFetching** (boolean) - `true` if the `fetchStatus` is `fetching`. - **isPaused** (boolean) - `true` if the `fetchStatus` is `paused`. - **isRefetching** (boolean) - `true` when a background refetch is in-flight (`isFetching && !isPending`). This excludes the initial `pending` state. - **isLoading** (boolean) - `true` when the very first fetch for a query is in-flight (`isFetching && isPending`). - **isInitialLoading** (boolean) - **Deprecated.** This is an alias for `isLoading` and will be removed in future versions. - **isEnabled** (boolean) - `true` if this query observer is currently enabled and can perform fetches. - **failureCount** (number) - The cumulative count of consecutive failures for the query. Resets to `0` upon a successful fetch. - **failureReason** (null | TError) - The error object associated with the most recent failure that triggered a retry attempt. Resets to `null` upon a successful fetch. - **errorUpdateCount** (number) - The total number of times an error has been set for this query. - **refetch** ((options?: { throwOnError?: boolean, cancelRefetch?: boolean }) => Promise) - A function to manually trigger a refetch of the query. - `throwOnError` (boolean) - Optional - If `true`, any error during refetching will be thrown. Otherwise, it will only be logged. - `cancelRefetch` (boolean) - Optional - Defaults to `true`. If `true`, a currently running request will be cancelled before a new one is made. If `false`, no refetch will occur if a request is already in progress. - **promise** (Promise) - A stable `Promise` that will resolve with the query's data once available. Requires the `experimental_prefetchInRender` feature flag on `QueryClient`. ### Response Example ```json { "status": "success", "isPending": false, "isSuccess": true, "isError": false, "isLoadingError": false, "isRefetchError": false, "data": { "id": "item-123", "value": "Fetched Data" }, "dataUpdatedAt": 1678886400000, "error": null, "errorUpdatedAt": 0, "isStale": false, "isPlaceholderData": false, "isFetched": true, "isFetchedAfterMount": true, "fetchStatus": "idle", "isFetching": false, "isPaused": false, "isRefetching": false, "isLoading": false, "isInitialLoading": false, "isEnabled": true, "failureCount": 0, "failureReason": null, "errorUpdateCount": 0, "refetch": "[function_reference]", "promise": "[promise_reference]" } ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Transform useQuery with object argument - TypeScript Source: https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/packages/query-codemods/src/v5/keep-previous-data/README.md Example of a useQuery call where the first argument is an object expression; this usage will be transformed by the codemod. Dependencies: @tanstack/query hooks in a TypeScript React codebase. Input: object expression with queryKey, queryFn, and options; Output: updated syntax after codemod. Limitation: only when the first argument is an object expression. ```TypeScript const { data } = useQuery({ queryKey: ['posts'], queryFn: queryFn, keepPreviousData: true, }) ``` -------------------------------- ### useSuspenseQuery Hook Basic Usage Source: https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/react/reference/useSuspenseQuery.md Demonstrates basic usage of useSuspenseQuery hook to fetch data with Suspense integration. The hook accepts options similar to useQuery but excludes throwOnError, enabled, and placeholderData. Returns a result object where data is guaranteed to be defined and status is either 'success' or 'error'. ```typescript const result = useSuspenseQuery(options) ``` -------------------------------- ### Fetch Data with `useQuery` Hook in Vue Component Source: https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/packages/vue-query/README.md Demonstrates how to use the `useQuery` hook within a Vue 3 `setup` function to fetch and manage asynchronous data. It defines a query by specifying a `queryKey` for caching and a `queryFn` to retrieve the data. ```tsx import { defineComponent } from 'vue' import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/vue-query' export default defineComponent({ name: 'MyComponent', setup() { const query = useQuery({ queryKey: ['todos'], queryFn: getTodos }) return { query, } }, }) ``` -------------------------------- ### Initialize a basic useQuery hook in TanStack Vue Query Source: https://github.com/tanstack/query/blob/main/docs/framework/vue/guides/queries.md This TypeScript snippet demonstrates the fundamental usage of the `useQuery` hook from `@tanstack/vue-query`. It defines a query with a unique `queryKey` for caching and identification, and a `queryFn` responsible for fetching the actual data. The `result` object will contain the query's state, data, and error information. ```ts import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/vue-query' const result = useQuery({ queryKey: ['todos'], queryFn: fetchTodoList }) ```
